rationale: dim1=1: treatment case with p=58, which falls in the 50<=p<70 band. dim2=0: top-ranked hypothesis is bad-debt under-reserving (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent vs AR growth); ground truth is deferred rebate-cost recognition (incl. side agreements) and rebates misclassified as SG&A rather than revenue reductions (AAER-3741). The doubtful-accounts allowance is not the correct account area for either truth mechanism (rebate accruals/revenue reductions/SG&A-COGS); SG&A appears only incidentally in affected_line_items while the mechanism narrative is entirely about bad-debt reserves. Coverage note: hypothesis #2 (premature revenue recognition/AR spike) loosely touches the revenue_recognition scheme type, and hypothesis #3 (COGS-vs-opex reclassification behind the 57.7%->48.4% margin swing) is the closest to the true rebate-cost misclassification, but per rubric only the top-ranked hypothesis is scored. dim3=0: top hypothesis maps to omission-estimate (reserve release/under-accrual); answer-key genre is active(cost); wrong genre. dim4=3: evidence quotes specific provided data points with values, accessions, and locations (allowance trend 2007-08-31 294.1M -> 2011-02-28 150.4M; AR 2.11B -> 3.22B; FY2009/FY2010 gross margin 57.7% vs 48.4%; 2010-05-31 AR spike 4.38B vs adjacent quarters; recast 8-Ks of 2011-04-08 followed by 424B5/FWP/424B2 on 2011-04-12/13), combined coherently across multiple years and statement lines. Consistency rule satisfied: p=58 with risk_tier=elevated (watch/elevated required), so no cap. Auditor flag: value 193810011.85 appears both as 2010-08-31 allowance (CL6) and Q4 FY2010 NetIncomeLoss (CL5) - possible copy artifact to verify against provided series. memorization_suspect_condition2=false: reasoning cites concrete provided data content rather than bare document lists, and mentions no post-cutoff facts (no SEC order/restatement/enforcement references; cited 8-Ks are within documents_used).
